text: ‘One who bore witness of God’s word’; others would understand this as meaning ‘the man who has now borne record of God’s word’, and as referring to the Apocalypse itself. But a comparison of 1.9; 6.9, and 20.4 below suggests that the verb here means bearing witness to God’s word in face of persecution (cf. I Tim. 6.13); in which case the Apostle is identifying himself as an eye-witness of the gospel story and a confessor for the faith.
Orichalc, an unidentified metal of the ancient world.
This may refer to the angel guardians of the churches, or perhaps to their bishops.
